concentration.headline: Primate city dominance — one city overwhelms all others
concentration.body: Bangkok accounts for a striking 58.5% of tracked urban population across 390 cities in Thailand. This level of primacy (59%) is characteristic of rapidly urbanising or historically centralised economies where one city concentrates political, economic and cultural power.

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Bangkok & Pak Kret
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Bangkok and Pak Kret (18 km apart, combined population 18,197,272). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 18 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Thailand exhibits strong primate city dominance: Bangkok holds 58.5% of tracked urban population — a striking concentration. The gravity network confirms this: connections involving Bangkok dominate, with Bangkok → Pak Kret among secondary links. This pattern is characteristic of historically centralised or rapidly urbanising economies.
